title: DynaMiCS: Fine-Tuning LLMs with Performance Constraints Using Dynamic Mixtures
gist: Apple's DynaMiCS recasts multi-domain LLM fine-tuning as a constrained optimization problem, using short probing runs to estimate a cross-domain slope matrix and compute mixture weights that improve target domains while keeping constrained-domain (general knowledge, instruction-following, safety) losses below reference levels, beating fixed-mixture baselines at lower cost.

insights.claim: DynaMiCS casts multi-domain fine-tuning of LLMs as a constrained optimization problem, explicitly enforcing preservation of capabilities like general knowledge, instruction following, and safety evaluations rather than relying on fixed heuristics.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Explicitly preserving safety and instruction-following during fine-tuning is a practical need for anyone shipping customized models, a recurring pain point for enterprise LLM adopters.
insights.quote: We propose DynaMiCS, a dynamic mixture optimizer that casts multi-domain fine-tuning as a constrained optimization problem.

insights.claim: At each update DynaMiCS runs short domain-specific probing to estimate a slope matrix of local cross-domain effects, capturing how training on each dataset affects each evaluation domain, then optimizes mixture weights over the probability simplex.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Modeling cross-domain interference directly is a mechanism competitors can adopt; the slope-matrix idea is the technical edge worth watching.
insights.quote: DynaMiCS performs short domain-specific probing runs to estimate a slope matrix of local cross-domain effects

insights.claim: DynaMiCS achieves stronger target-domain improvements and higher constraint satisfaction than fixed-mixture baselines at lower computational cost, and without reference models, per-example scoring, or manually tuned mixture weights.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Dropping reference models and manual tuning lowers the cost and expertise barrier to safe fine-tuning, favoring wider on-device and enterprise adoption.
insights.quote: at lower computational cost and without reference models, per-example scoring, or manually tuned mixture weights

insights.claim: Existing data mixing strategies rely on fixed heuristics or adaptive rules that cannot explicitly enforce preservation of constrained capabilities, which DynaMiCS is designed to overcome.
insights.speaker: Apple ML Research
insights.so_what: Frames incumbent heuristic mixing as fundamentally unable to guarantee capability retention, a differentiator for Apple's fine-tuning stack.
insights.quote: Existing data mixing strategies rely on fixed heuristics or adaptive rules that cannot explicitly enforce preservation of such capabilities.
insights.locator: Multi-domain fine-tuning of large language models
